label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is NOT an advantage of the algorithm described in the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. I need to figure out which one isn't an advantage. First, I should go through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The monitor "watches" deceleration and distance from a light, guesses which cars will break the Highway Code. It provides two seconds' warning if someone is about to cross a crossroads, giving time to brake. Professor How says smart cars might use this to help drivers foresee and avoid accidents. The algorithm was tested in Virginia and accurately identified violators within a couple of seconds of a red light, allowing others to react if alerted. It's better than other models because it has fewer false alarms, which is important for systems guiding human drivers. The paper is going to be published in a journal. Now, looking at the options: A) It will raise the alarm when the driver breaks the traffic rules. B) It usually produces fewer false alarms. C) It helps drivers avoid potential accidents by judging the threats. D) The monitor of this algorithm can judge the traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. The passage mentions that the algorithm accurately identifies potential violators (so it can raise an alarm when someone breaks rules - A seems like an advantage). B is directly stated as an advantage because it has fewer false alarms. C is mentioned because the system helps foresee and avoid accidents by judging threats. D says the monitor judges traffic condition from the lights of other vehicles. The passage says the monitor "watches" deceleration and distance from a light. So it's tracking deceleration (how fast a car is slowing down) and the distance to the light. It doesn't mention using the lights (like brake lights or turn signals) of other vehicles.
